source_file: Original draft for FlutterCon USA 2026 roundtable
question: Q4: Flutter in an Agentic Coding World

sections.prompt: How many of you feel AI coding agents are changing your work from writing every line to directing, reviewing, and integrating?
sections.hint: Recent mainstream coverage of AI coding describes developers spending less time typing code and more time supervising agents, shaping intent, and inspecting strange-but-useful outputs.
no_hands: Why will traditional programming skill still matter?
Which parts of Flutter are hard for agents to abstract away?
What framework qualities become more important when AI writes more code?
hands_up: What parts of framework knowledge feel easier to delegate to AI?
Does AI make it less risky to choose a framework with a learning curve?
Will hiring shift from framework experts to product/system problem-solvers?
How should Flutter developers describe their value if syntax matters less?
follow_ups: Does framework maturity matter more when agents generate large diffs?
Will teams standardize more or experiment more with frameworks?
What remains uniquely human in framework selection?

sections.prompt: Has anyone found AI agents useful in their Flutter work so far?
sections.hint: Examples: widgets, tests, refactors, navigation, state management wiring, docs, bug investigation, and the review skill needed to catch confident mistakes.
no_hands: Which Flutter tasks remain too context-heavy for agents?
What failure modes make you cautious?
What would you need before trusting an agent with larger changes?
hands_up: Which Flutter tasks do agents handle well today?
Where do they still make subtle mistakes?
How do you review AI-generated Flutter code efficiently?
What guardrails make agentic coding safer?
follow_ups: Are agents better at greenfield work or maintaining existing apps?
What tests catch the most AI-generated Flutter issues?
How do agents change the role of code review?

sections.prompt: Who here thinks that in agentic workflows, verification is becoming the core developer skill?
sections.hint: Recent software-engineering research and media coverage point in the same direction: as agents handle more implementation, humans spend more effort validating correctness, maintainability, security, and product fit.
no_hands: Do you think writing from scratch is still the core skill?
Which writing skills should developers keep practicing?
What risks come from treating verification as a separate skill from implementation?
hands_up: What makes someone good at reviewing AI-generated Flutter code?
Which debugging skills are hardest for AI to replace?
How do you teach code reading to beginners if agents write much of the first draft?
What Flutter-specific bugs require real framework understanding?
follow_ups: Should interviews test debugging more than implementation?
How do you practice code review without a team?
What is one debugging habit AI users need?

sections.prompt: Anyone think judgment is becoming the real career bet: knowing what to build, not just how to ask AI to build it?
sections.hint: Examples: choosing problems, shaping user flows, deciding boundaries, managing complexity, and knowing when not to automate.
no_hands: Where does syntax or framework detail still decide project success?
Can architecture judgment be learned through AI-assisted building?
What should juniors do before they can make good architecture decisions?
hands_up: What product or architecture decisions does AI still struggle with?
How does Flutter help or hurt product experimentation?
What should developers learn to guide agents instead of only prompting them?
How do you know when AI is solving the wrong problem?
follow_ups: What question should developers ask before asking AI to code?
How can Flutter developers show product judgment in real work?
What architecture mistake can AI make faster than a human?

wrap_up: In an agentic coding world, what should Flutter developers become excellent at?
Does AI make Flutter more valuable, less valuable, or simply different?
What is one agentic workflow you would teach every Flutter developer?
